ES How to help
← Back to the observatory How we work

Methodology & corrections

This observatory documents verifiable breaches by the CNE. The minimum standard is simple: every claim must have a primary source or a verifiable archive. When a data point comes only from secondary sources, it is marked Reported; breaches of the law are tagged with their article.

Evidence status

Each strong claim carries one of these tags:

  • Verified A fact with a primary source and a public archive anyone can review.
  • Reported A data point from a reliable secondary source (e.g. detention figures from an NGO), not from our own primary source.
  • Breach of law An established legal breach, cited with its article (e.g. Art. 146 LOPRE).

Source tiers

Not all sources carry equal weight. We label each one by type:

  • Official — CNE, TSJ, Electoral Gazette, international bodies.
  • Observer — missions and panels with published methodology (Carter Center, UN Panel, EU, OAS).
  • NGO — reports with verifiable methodology (HRW, OHCHR, Amnesty).
  • Academic — independent analysis with a citable method (Tao, Kronick, Gelman, Mebane).
  • Citizen — verifiable tally sheets and databases (ResultadosConVzla).

How to verify it yourself

  1. Open ResultadosConVzla and find your polling table.
  2. Cross-check the QR code and serial on the tally sheet against the published image.
  3. Compare the sum of tables with the official result. The CNE never published the per-table breakdown, so it cannot be cross-checked — that is the point.

Corrections

If you find an error, send the corrected source and a short explanation via GitHub Issues. Corrections are logged with date, reason and reference. Content is under a Creative Commons license and the code is open source.

Update log

Last general verification: 2026-06-06.
